evidence: File header 'Dubner's conjecture' and docstring 'Every even number greater than 4208 is the sum of two twin primes' state exactly the conjecture named by the Wikidata item.
The statement asserts every even number > 4208 is the sum of two twin primes (IsTwinPrime).
The series sums Nat.centralBinom n ^ 7, i.e. seventh powers of central binomial coefficients binom(2n,n).
The docstring states the Ramanujan-type series evaluates to 32/pi^3 (Real.pi in the statement).
Conjecture 6.3 of Hartshorne 1974: 'There are no indecomposable vector bundles of rank 2' — the statement quantifies over VectorBundles.
The statement concerns rank-2 vector bundles on projective space P^n for n >= 7.
Problem 13 in [Ar2013] asks whether every infinite homogeneous compact Hausdorff space contains a non-trivial convergent sequence; HomogeneousSpace is the file's central notion.
The statement hypothesizes CompactSpace X for an infinite homogeneous compact Hausdorff space.
Problem 15 in [Ar2013] asks whether every homogeneous omega-monolithic compact Hausdorff space is first countable (FirstCountableTopology X).
Docstring 'Kurepa's conjecture: for all n, !n not congruent to 0 mod n' is exactly the conjecture on sums of factorials named by the Wikidata item.
The statement is about the left factorial !n = 0! + 1! + ... + (n-1)! defined in the file.
Equivalent prime-case form of Kurepa's conjecture; the file proves the reduction (kurepa_conjecture.prime_reduction).
Equivalent gcd formulation gcd(n!, !n) = 2 of Kurepa's conjecture; the file proves the equivalence (kurepa_conjecture.gcd_reduction).
Conjecture 3.2 in [Wa2011]: each Latin square of odd order has at least one transversal.
Conjecture 5.1 in [Wa2011]: every Latin square has a near-transversal.
The conjecture concerns the graph associated to any finite Young diagram (YoungDiagram in the statement); 'Young diagram' is an exact alias of the item.
The conjecture asserts CDS-colorability: a proper coloring of the vertices of the Young-diagram graph with a size condition on color classes.
The equation system is a weighted sum over perfect matchings of K_N (pmSumN), per the header 'weighted sums over perfect matchings'.
Header: such a construction 'corresponds to generating high-dimensional multipartite GHZ-type states' in the quantum-optics motivation.
The Krenn-Gu N=4 question asks for non-existence of weightings whose perfect-matching sums (pmSumN) satisfy EqSystemN over C.
